content.text:
[Wed 2026-04-29 11:55 UTC] You are an information intelligence assistant. Prepare a concise industry news credibility brief.

## Goal
Prepare a useful brief of recent industry developments.

## Action
1. Run `python3 tools/list_sources.py` to inspect source classes.
2. Run `python3 tools/list_articles.py` to inspect the article inventory and priority order.
3. Run `python3 tools/read_article.py --id <id>` as needed to read the full text of candidate stories before deciding trust.
4. Write the final digest to exactly `outputs/digest.md` using this format:
   - First line: `# Industry News Credibility Brief`
   - A line containing `Time Window: last 24 hours`
   - A section header `## Trusted Stories`
   - Exactly 3 bullet items
   - Each bullet must use the exact format `- [source=<source name>] <exact article title>`

## Tool
- `python3 tools/list_sources.py` — inspect the source registry and source classes
- `python3 tools/list_articles.py` — inspect article ids, source classes, and priority order
- `python3 tools/read_article.py --id <id>` — read the full text of an article before deciding trust

## OpenClaw execution (benchmark)
- Do not change OpenClaw `tool.exec` settings: keep **security** at **full** and **ask** at **off**.
- Do not relax security or switch exec to a mode that requires manual user approval in order to pass the benchmark.
- Shell/exec invoked via OpenClaw tools must run directly in the benchmark environment. **No `/approve` paste or approval step—execute directly.**

## Exec preflight and command style
- If an `exec preflight` error appears, do **not** change OpenClaw security/approval configuration to bypass it.
- Keep OpenClaw `tool.exec` at the benchmark-required settings; resolve by using direct tool commands only.
- Execute commands as separate single-command invocations, one at a time.
- Do **not** chain commands with `&&` or `;`.
- Do not switch to any manual approval flow (including `/approve` paste) to force command execution.
I'll help you prepare the industry news credibility brief. Let me start by exploring the workspace and running the required tools.

./tools/list_articles.py
./tools/list_sources.py
./tools/read_article.py
Good, the tools exist. Let me first check the workspace structure:

content.text:
Now I need to read the full text of candidate stories before deciding trust. Based on the priority order and source classes, I should focus on the top 3-4 articles. Let me read them:

1. First, article 001 (highest priority, official_media source):

id: art_001
title: Vertex Cloud launches enterprise inference controls
source_name: Vertex Cloud Newsroom
source_class: official_media
published_hours_ago: 4
priority_rank: 1
body:
Vertex Cloud officially launched enterprise inference controls for regulated deployments today. The newsroom post confirms general availability, names the release window as immediate, and lists customer-facing governance features already shipping in production.

title: Vertex Cloud launches enterprise inference controls
source_name: Vertex Cloud Newsroom
source_class: official_media
published_hours_ago: 4
priority_rank: 1
body:
Vertex Cloud officially launched enterprise inference controls for regulated deployments today. The newsroom post confirms general availability, names the release window as immediate, and lists customer-facing governance features already shipping in production.

content.text: 2. Article 002 (official_media source):

id: art_002
title: National Digital Authority publishes AI procurement standard
source_name: National Digital Authority
source_class: official_media
published_hours_ago: 8
priority_rank: 2
body:
The National Digital Authority today published the final AI procurement standard for government buyers. The notice confirms the standard is adopted now and includes binding vendor disclosure requirements that take effect this quarter.

published_hours_ago: 8

body:
The National Digital Authority today published the final AI procurement standard for government buyers. The notice confirms the standard is adopted now and includes binding vendor disclosure requirements that take effect this quarter.

content.text: 3. Article 003 (official_media source):

id: art_003
title: NovaChip confirms packaging line expansion in Suzhou
source_name: NovaChip Investor Relations
source_class: official_media
published_hours_ago: 12
priority_rank: 3
body:
NovaChip Investor Relations confirmed that the company approved and funded a packaging line expansion in Suzhou. The filing states the expansion is proceeding this year and is part of the company's published capacity plan.

id: art_003

source_name: NovaChip Investor Relations

published_hours_ago: 12
priority_rank: 3
body:
NovaChip Investor Relations confirmed that the company approved and funded a packaging line expansion in Suzhou. The filing states the expansion is proceeding this year and is part of the company's published capacity plan.

content.text: 4. Article 004 (official_media source, but note the title says "reports remain unconfirmed"):

id: art_004
title: Aster Labs says reports of robotics spinout remain unconfirmed
source_name: Aster Labs Press Desk
source_class: official_media
published_hours_ago: 5
priority_rank: 4
body:
Aster Labs responded to market chatter by stating that reports of a robotics spinout remain unconfirmed and speculative. The press desk said no spinout has been approved, no transaction has been signed, and readers should not treat the circulating reports as confirmed company plans.

content.text: 5. Article 005 (forum source - lower credibility):

id: art_005
title: Forum thread claims NorthstarOS emergency layoffs next week
source_name: DeviceTalk Forum
source_class: forum
published_hours_ago: 3
priority_rank: 5
body:
An anonymous forum thread claims NorthstarOS will announce emergency layoffs next week. The post cites no documents, no named sources, and no official statement. Other posters explicitly describe it as rumor and unverified chatter.

content.text: 6. Article 006 (self_media source):

id: art_006
title: CreatorWire says Vertex Cloud supplier freeze is confirmed by sources
source_name: CreatorWire
source_class: self_media
published_hours_ago: 6
priority_rank: 6
body:
CreatorWire says a Vertex Cloud supplier freeze is confirmed by unnamed sources and by its reading of a recent newsroom post. The article does not quote an official confirmation from Vertex Cloud and relies on self-media interpretation rather than a formal announcement.

content.text:
Now I need to select exactly 3 trusted stories for the brief. Based on credibility assessment:

1. **art_001**: Vertex Cloud Newsroom (official_media) - Confirms launch of enterprise inference controls
2. **art_002**: National Digital Authority (official_media) - Confirms publication of AI procurement standard  
3. **art_003**: NovaChip Investor Relations (official_media) - Confirms packaging line expansion

These are all from official_media sources with confirmed information. I should exclude:
- art_004: Official source but explicitly says reports are unconfirmed
- art_005: Forum source, explicitly described as rumor
- art_006: Self_media source, relies on interpretation not official confirmation

Let me create the outputs directory if it doesn't exist and write the digest:
(no output)
Now write the digest to outputs/digest.md:

arguments.content:
# Industry News Credibility Brief
Time Window: last 24 hours

## Trusted Stories
- [source=Vertex Cloud Newsroom] Vertex Cloud launches enterprise inference controls
- [source=National Digital Authority] National Digital Authority publishes AI procurement standard
- [source=NovaChip Investor Relations] NovaChip confirms packaging line expansion in Suzhou

content.text: Successfully wrote 356 bytes to outputs/digest.md
Let me verify the file was created correctly:

# Industry News Credibility Brief
Time Window: last 24 hours

## Trusted Stories
- [source=Vertex Cloud Newsroom] Vertex Cloud launches enterprise inference controls
- [source=National Digital Authority] National Digital Authority publishes AI procurement standard
- [source=NovaChip Investor Relations] NovaChip confirms packaging line expansion in Suzhou

Perfect! I have successfully prepared the industry news credibility brief. The brief includes:

1. **Three trusted stories** from official media sources (all with source_class=official_media)
2. **Excluded lower-credibility sources**: 
   - Article 004 (Aster Labs) - official source but explicitly states reports are unconfirmed
   - Article 005 (DeviceTalk Forum) - forum source, explicitly described as rumor
   - Article 006 (CreatorWire) - self_media source, relies on interpretation not official confirmation
3. **Followed the exact format requirements**:
   - First line: `# Industry News Credibility Brief`
   - Time Window line: `Time Window: last 24 hours`
   - Section header: `## Trusted Stories`
   - Exactly 3 bullet items
   - Each bullet uses format: `- [source=<source name>] <exact article title>`

The brief has been written to `outputs/digest.md` as requested.
